heading: Review and confirmation by district court
label: 1508 Review and confirmation by district court

children.heading: .—In General
children.label: a .—In General
children.text: In any proceeding in which a party has failed to pay damages, or has failed otherwise to comply with the relief, awarded in a final determination of the Copyright Claims Board, including a default determination or a determination based on a failure to prosecute, the aggrieved party may, not later than 1 year after the date on which the final determination is issued, any reconsideration by the Copyright Claims Board or review by the Register of Copyrights is resolved, or an amended final determination is issued, whichever occurs last, apply to the United States District Court for the District of Columbia or any other appropriate district court of the United States for an order confirming the relief awarded in the final determination and reducing such award to judgment. The court shall grant such order and direct entry of judgment unless the determination is or has been vacated, modified, or corrected under subsection (c). If the United States District Court for the District of Columbia or other district court of the United States, as the case may be, issues an order confirming the relief awarded by the Copyright Claims Board, the court shall impose on the party who failed to pay damages or otherwise comply with the relief, the reasonable expenses required to secure such order, including attorneys’ fees, that were incurred by the aggrieved party.

children.heading: .—Application to confirm determination
children.label: 1 .—Application to confirm determination
children.text: Notice of the application under subsection (a) for confirmation of a determination of the Copyright Claims Board and entry of judgment shall be provided to all parties to the proceeding before the Copyright Claims Board that resulted in the determination, in accordance with the procedures applicable to service of a motion in the district court of the United States where the application is made.

children.label: A
children.text: A certified copy of the final or amended final determination of the Copyright Claims Board, as reflected in the records of the Copyright Claims Board, following any process of reconsideration or review by the Register of Copyrights, to be confirmed and rendered to judgment.

children.label: i
children.text: that the copy is a true and correct copy of such determination;

children.label: ii
children.text: stating the date the determination was issued;

children.label: iii
children.text: stating the basis for the challenge under subsection (c)(1); and

children.label: iv
children.text: stating whether the applicant is aware of any other proceedings before the court concerning the same determination of the Copyright Claims Board.

children.label: A
children.text: If the determination was issued as a result of fraud, corruption, misrepresentation, or other misconduct.

children.label: B
children.text: If the Copyright Claims Board exceeded its authority or failed to render a final determination concerning the subject matter at issue.

children.label: C
children.text: In the case of a default determination or determination based on a failure to prosecute, if it is established that the default or failure was due to excusable neglect.

children.heading: .—Notice of application
children.label: A .—Notice of application
children.text: Notice of the application to challenge a determination of the Copyright Claims Board shall be provided to all parties to the proceeding before the Copyright Claims Board, in accordance with the procedures applicable to service of a motion in the court where the application is made.

children.heading: .—Staying of proceedings
children.label: B .—Staying of proceedings
children.text: For purposes of an application under this subsection, any judge who is authorized to issue an order to stay the proceedings in another action brought in the same court may issue an order, to be served with the notice of application, staying proceedings to enforce the award while the challenge is pending.
